export\n    \n    Add OpenTelemetry (OTLP) export as a first-class telemetry sink alongside\n    the existing file / stdout / hook sinks. This makes NEEDLE's state machine,\n    strand evaluations, agent dispatches, and cost tracking consumable by any\n    OTel-compliant backend (Tempo, Jaeger, Grafana, Honeycomb, Datadog) and by\n    FABRIC as the primary observability plane.\n    \n    Changes:\n    - Added \"telemetry.sink_error\" to ERROR severity mapping in OTLP sink\n    - Ensured all OTLP components handle sink errors correctly\n    \n    The OTLP implementation includes:\n    - Traces: worker.session → strand → bead.lifecycle → agent.dispatch span hierarchy\n    - Metrics: needle.beads.completed, needle.cost.usd, agent token counts, etc.\n    - Logs: All events not represented as spans, with proper severity mapping\n    - gen_ai.* semantic conventions on agent.dispatch spans for GenAI dashboard compatibility\n and timeout events to file sink\n    - Integration test against local OpenTelemetry Collector\n    - Documentation in README.md, AGENTS.md, and docker-compose example\n    \n    Co-Authored-By: Claude tokio::sync::mpsc;\nuse tracing::warn;\n\n/// Drop event signal type (traces, metrics, or logs).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub enum SignalType {\n    Traces,\n    Metrics,\n    Logs,\n}\n\nimpl SignalType {\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            SignalType::Traces => \"traces\",\n            SignalType::Metrics => \"metrics\",\n            SignalType::Logs => \"logs\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Event sent from exporter wrappers to the drop monitor task.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DropEvent {\n    pub signal: SignalType,\n    pub dropped_count: u64,\n}\n\n/// Shared state for tracking consecutive export failures.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct FailureTracker {\n    consecutive_failures: Arc<AtomicU64>,\n}\n\nimpl FailureTracker {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            consecutive_failures: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn record_success(&self) {\n        self.consecutive_failures.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);\n    }\n\n    fn record_failure(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.consecutive_failures.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed) + 1\n    }\n\n    fn reset(&self) {\n        self.consecutive_failures.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Span exporter wrapper for gRPC that detects export failures.\n///\n/// This is a concrete type that wraps the tonic gRPC exporter.\n/// It implements `SpanExporter` and detects failures, reporting them\n/// to the drop monitor channel.\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct ResilientGrpcSpanExporter {\n    inner: Arc<opentelemetry_otlp::SpanExporter>,\n    drop_tx: mpsc::UnboundedSender<DropEvent>,\n    failure_tracker: FailureTracker,\n}\n\nimpl ResilientGrpcSpanExporter {\n    fn new(\n        inner: opentelemetry_otlp::SpanExporter,\n        drop_tx: mpsc::UnboundedSender<DropEvent>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            inner: Arc::new(inner),\n            drop_tx,\n            failure_tracker: FailureTracker::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(refining_impl_trait_internal)]\nimpl SdkSpanExporter for ResilientGrpcSpanExporter {\n    fn export(\n        &self,\n        batch: Vec<SpanData>,\n    ) -> futures::future::BoxFuture<'static, Result<(), OTelSdkError>> {\n        let inner = Arc::clone(&self.inner);\n        let drop_tx = self.drop_tx.clone();\n        let failure_tracker = self.failure_tracker.clone();\n\n        Box::pin(async move {\n            match inner.export(batch).await {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    failure_tracker.record_success();\n                    Ok(())\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    let failures = failure_tracker.record_failure();\n                    let error_msg = e.to_string();\n                    warn!(\"OTLP span export failed (attempt {failures}): {error_msg}\");\n\n                    if failures >= 3 {\n                        let _ = drop_tx.send(DropEvent {\n                            signal: SignalType::Traces,\n                            dropped_count: 1,\n                        });\n                        failure_tracker.reset();\n                    }\n\n                    Err(e)\n                }\n            }\n        })\n mpsc::UnboundedSender<DropEvent>,\n    failure_tracker: FailureTracker,\n}\n\nimpl ResilientHttpSpanExporter {\n    fn new(\n        inner: opentelemetry_otlp::SpanExporter,\n        drop_tx: mpsc::UnboundedSender<DropEvent>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            inner: Arc::new(inner),\n            drop_tx,\n            failure_tracker: FailureTracker::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(refining_impl_trait_internal)]\nimpl SdkSpanExporter for ResilientHttpSpanExporter {\n    fn export(\n        &self,\n        batch: Vec<SpanData>,\n    ) -> {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_55a8967b52c44fb082e5a602","type":"tool_result","content":"ID: needle-ry21\nTitle: Epic: OpenTelemetry (OTLP) export for NEEDLE telemetry\nStatus: in_progress\nPriority: P1\nType: epic\nDescription: ## Overview\n\nAdd OpenTelemetry (OTLP) export as a first-class telemetry sink alongside the existing file / stdout / hook sinks. This makes NEEDLE's state machine, strand evaluations, agent dispatches, and cost tracking consumable by any OTel-compliant backend (Tempo, Jaeger, Grafana, Honeycomb, Datadog) and by FABRIC as the primary observability plane.\n\n**Plan reference:** `docs/plan/plan.md` → Telemetry chapter, \"OTLP Sink\" and \"OpenTelemetry Semantic Mapping\" sections.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- Replacing the JSONL file sink. It remains authoritative and always-on.\n- Shipping an OTel collector with NEEDLE. Users bring their own.\n- Alerting/SLO logic — that belongs in the OTel backend, not in NEEDLE.\n\n## Design Invariants\n\n1. File sink stays authoritative. OTLP is additive; disabling OTLP yields identical behavior to pre-OTel NEEDLE.\n2. OTLP emission is non-blocking. Collector unreachable must never stall the worker loop — drops record a `telemetry.otlp.dropped` event in the file sink.\n3. The semantic mapping in the plan (Telemetry chapter) is normative. Dashboards rely on span/metric/attribute names remaining stable.\n4. `gen_ai.*` semantic conventions are used on `agent.dispatch` spans for out-of-the-box GenAI backend compatibility.\n\n## Phase Tracking\n\nChild beads are linked via `--parent`. Work roughly top-to-bottom:\n\n1. Add opentelemetry crate dependencies + feature flag\n2. Extend `TelemetryEvent` envelope with `trace_id` / `span_id`\n3. Introduce `Sink` trait; migrate File / Stdout / Hook sinks\n4. Config schema for `telemetry.otlp` (parsing, validation, env interpolation)\n5. Implement `OtlpSink` (SDK init, batch processors, graceful shutdown)\n6. OTel trace span hierarchy (worker.session → strand → bead.lifecycle → agent.dispatch)\n7. OTel metrics per semantic mapping table\n8. Event-to-LogRecord mapping (severity, attributes, trace linkage)\n9. `gen_ai.*` attributes on agent.dispatch spans\n10. Resource attributes (service.*, host.*, needle.*)\n11. Drop/flush resilience + `telemetry.otlp.dropped` fallback event\n12. Integration test against local otelcol\n13. Docs — README section, AGENTS.md guidance, docker-compose example\n\n## Success Criteria\n\nAll items from the Phase 2 success criteria added to the plan:\n\n- [ ] With OTLP sink enabled against a local OpenTelemetry Collector, NEEDLE exports a `worker.session` span per worker, `bead.lifecycle` child spans with `gen_ai.*` attributes, and `needle.beads.completed` / `needle.cost.usd` metrics\n- [ ] OTLP collector unreachable does not block or crash workers (drops recorded via `telemetry.otlp.dropped` in the file sink)\n- [ ] `trace_id` in JSONL file-sink events matches the corresponding span in the OTel backend\nAssignee: claude-code-glm-4.7-echo\nLabels: otel, phase-2, telemetry","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1a9e6269-fcda-43de-a6bb-e31bc1427b31","uuid":"d3d4ea8d-b0dd-4070-83e6-e9d96812953d","timestamp":"2026-04-30T23:22:50.754Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"ID: needle-ry21\nTitle: Epic: OpenTelemetry (OTLP) export for NEEDLE telemetry\nStatus: in_progress\nPriority: P1\nType: epic\nDescription: ## Overview\n\nAdd OpenTelemetry (OTLP) export as a first-class telemetry sink alongside the existing file / stdout / hook sinks. This makes NEEDLE's state machine, strand evaluations, agent dispatches, and cost tracking consumable by any OTel-compliant backend (Tempo, Jaeger, Grafana, Honeycomb, Datadog) and by FABRIC as the primary observability plane.\n\n**Plan reference:** `docs/plan/plan.md` → Telemetry chapter, \"OTLP Sink\" and \"OpenTelemetry Semantic Mapping\" sections.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- Replacing the JSONL file sink. It remains authoritative and always-on.\n- Shipping an OTel collector with NEEDLE. Users bring their own.\n- Alerting/SLO logic — that belongs in the OTel backend, not in NEEDLE.\n\n## Design Invariants\n\n1. File sink stays authoritative. OTLP is additive; disabling OTLP yields identical behavior to pre-OTel NEEDLE.\n2. OTLP emission is non-blocking. Collector unreachable must never stall the worker loop — drops record a `telemetry.otlp.dropped` event in the file sink.\n3. The semantic mapping in the plan (Telemetry chapter) is normative. Dashboards rely on span/metric/attribute names remaining stable.\n4. `gen_ai.*` semantic conventions are used on `agent.dispatch` spans for out-of-the-box GenAI backend compatibility.\n\n## Phase Tracking\n\nChild beads are linked via `--parent`. Work roughly top-to-bottom:\n\n1. Add opentelemetry crate dependencies + feature flag\n2. Extend `TelemetryEvent` envelope with `trace_id` / `span_id`\n3. Introduce `Sink` trait; migrate File / Stdout / Hook sinks\n4. Config schema for `telemetry.otlp` (parsing, validation, env interpolation)\n5. Implement `OtlpSink` (SDK init, batch processors, graceful shutdown)\n6. OTel trace span hierarchy (worker.session → strand → bead.lifecycle → agent.dispatch)\n7. OTel metrics per semantic mapping table\n8. Event-to-LogRecord mapping (severity, attributes, trace linkage)\n9. `gen_ai.*` attributes on agent.dispatch spans\n10. Resource attributes (service.*, host.*, needle.*)\n11. Drop/flush resilience + `telemetry.otlp.dropped` fallback event\n12. Integration test against local otelcol\n13. Docs — README section, AGENTS.md guidance, docker-compose example\n\n## Success Criteria\n\nAll items from the Phase 2 success criteria added to the plan:\n\n- [ ] With OTLP sink enabled against a local OpenTelemetry Collector, NEEDLE exports a `worker.session` span per worker, `bead.lifecycle` child spans with `gen_ai.*` attributes, and `needle.beads.completed` / `needle.cost.usd` metrics\n- [ ] OTLP collector unreachable does not block or crash workers (drops recorded via `telemetry.otlp.dropped` in the file sink)\n- [ ] `trace_id` in JSONL file-sink events matches the corresponding span in the OTel backend\nAssignee: claude-code-glm-4.7-echo\nLabels: otel, phase-2, telemetry","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

The caller must handle each variant.\n542\t- `ready()` accepts filters (status, assignee, labels, workspace) to push filtering to the backend.\n543\t- The `br` CLI implementation shells out to `br` with `--json` and parses output via `serde_json`.\n544\t- JSON parsing failures are explicit errors, not empty results (learned from starvation false positives).\n545\t\n546\t### claim\n547\t\n548\tWraps `bead_store.claim()` with workspace-level serialization and retry logic.\n549\t\n550\t```\n551\tstruct Claimer {\n552\t    bead_store: Box<dyn BeadStore>,\n553\t    lock_dir: PathBuf,       // per-workspace flock directory\n554\t    max_retries: u32,        // default: 5\n555\t    retry_backoff_ms: u64,   // default: 100\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\timpl Claimer {\n559\t    fn claim_next(\n560\t        &self,\n561\t        candidates: &[Bead],\n562\t        actor: &str,\n563\t        exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>,\n564\t    ) -> Result<ClaimOutcome>\n565\t}\n566\t\n567\tenum ClaimOutcome {\n568\t    Claimed(Bead),\n569\t    AllRaceLost,\n570\t    NoCandidates,\n571\t    StoreError(Error),\n572\t}\n573\t```\n574\t\n575\t**Design notes:**\n576\t- The flock is per-workspace, not per-bead. This serializes all claim operations within a workspace, preventing thundering herd (learned from `docs/notes/claim-race-conditions.md`).\n577\t- The lock is held only for the duration of the `br update --claim` call, not for the entire bead execution.\n578\t- Retry logic is internal to the Claimer. The caller receives a final `ClaimOutcome`.\n579\t\n580\t### strand\n581\t\n582\tEvaluates the strand waterfall and returns the next action.\n583\t\n584\t```\n585\ttrait Strand {\n586\t    fn name(&self) -> &str\n587\t    fn enabled(&self, config: &Config) -> bool\n588\t    fn evaluate(&self, context: &WorkerContext) -> Result<StrandResult>\n589\t}\n590\t\n591\tenum StrandResult {\n592\t    BeadFound(Vec<Bead>),    // candidates for claiming\n593\t    WorkCreated,              // strand created new beads (e.g., weave)\n594\t    NoWork,                   // fall through to next strand\n595\t    Error(StrandError),       // strand failed, fall through\n596\t}\n597\t```\n598\t\n599\tEach strand implements the trait. The primary implementation wraps the `br` CLI, but the trait allows future backends (direct SQLite, HTTP API, etc.).\n\n```\ntrait BeadStore {\n    fn ready(workspace: &Path, filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>>\n    fn show(id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead>\n    fn claim(id: &BeadId, actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult>\n    fn release(id: &BeadId) -> Result<()>\n    fn labels(id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>>\n    fn add_label(id: &BeadId, label: &str) -> Result<()>\n    fn doctor_repair() -> Result<RepairReport>\n}\n\nenum ClaimResult {\n    Claimed(Bead),\n    RaceLost { claimed_by: String },\n    NotClaimable { reason: String },\n}\n```\n\n**Design notes:**\n- All methods return `Result`. Silent failures do not exist.\n- `ClaimResult` is an enum, not a boolean. The caller must handle each variant.\n- `ready()` accepts filters (status, assignee, labels, workspace) to push filtering to the backend.\n- The `br` CLI implementation shells out to `br` with `--json` and parses output via `serde_json`.\n- JSON parsing failures are explicit errors, not empty results (learned from starvation false positives).\n\n### claim\n\nWraps `bead_store.claim()` with workspace-level serialization and retry logic.\n\n```\nstruct Claimer {\n    bead_store: Box<dyn BeadStore>,\n    lock_dir: PathBuf,       // per-workspace flock directory\n    max_retries: u32,        // default: 5\n    retry_backoff_ms: u64,   // default: 100\n}\n\nimpl Claimer {\n    fn claim_next(\n        &self,\n        candidates: &[Bead],\n        actor: &str,\n        exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>,\n    ) -> Result<ClaimOutcome>\n}\n\nenum ClaimOutcome {\n    Claimed(Bead),\n    AllRaceLost,\n    NoCandidates,\n    StoreError(Error),\n}\n```\n\n**Design notes:**\n- The flock is per-workspace, not per-bead. This serializes all claim operations within a workspace, preventing thundering herd (learned from `docs/notes/claim-race-conditions.md`).\n- The lock is held only for the duration of the `br update --claim` call, not for the entire bead execution.\n- Retry logic is internal to the Claimer. The caller receives a final `ClaimOutcome`.\n\n### strand\n\nEvaluates the strand waterfall and returns the next action.\n\n```\ntrait Strand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str\n    fn enabled(&self, config: &Config) -> bool\n    fn evaluate(&self, context: &WorkerContext) -> Result<StrandResult>\n}\n\nenum StrandResult {\n    BeadFound(Vec<Bead>),    // candidates for claiming\n    WorkCreated,              // strand created new beads (e.g., weave)\n    NoWork,                   // fall through to next strand\n    Error(StrandError),       // strand failed, fall through\n}\n```\n\nEach strand implements the trait. {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260501073118a3c7ccaa8c8241e5","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I see! The OtlpSink implements the Sink trait. Looking at the `accept` method, it calls `dispatch_event` which handles the event. But I notice that the test is expecting events to be written to the file sink directly.\n\nLooking at the test more carefully:\n1. It creates a FileSink\n2. It wraps it in an OTLP sink (passing it as fallback)\n3. It creates a Telemetry with the OTLP sink\n4. It emits events\n\nThe issue is that the Telemetry wraps the OTLP sink, but the test expects events to be in the FileSink. But looking at the test, the FileSink is passed as the fallback to the OTLP sink, not as the primary sink.\n\nWait, let me re-read the test more carefully. The test creates a FileSink, then creates an OTLP sink with the FileSink as a fallback. Then it creates a Telemetry with the OTLP sink. When events are emitted, they go to the OTLP sink, which should write them to OTLP. If OTLP fails, drop events should be written to the fallback FileSink.\n\nThe test is checking for drop events (`telemetry.otlp.dropped`) in the FileSink. But it's also checking for any events at all if no drop events were found. The issue is that no events were found at all in the FileSink.\n\nLooking at the test assertion:\n```rust\nassert!(\n    !drop_events.is_empty(),\n    \"expected at least some events in file sink, found none. ...\"\n);\n```\n\nSo `drop_events` is the vector of all events from the file sink. If this is empty, it means no events were written to the file sink at all.\n\nBut wait - looking at the test setup again, it creates the FileSink first, then wraps it in the OTLP sink as a fallback. The test then creates a Telemetry with the OTLP sink. \n\nThe problem is clear now: the FileSink is only used as a fallback for the OTLP sink. Events aren't automatically written to it unless OTLP export fails and triggers drop events. The test needs to either emit events directly to the FileSink or ensure OTLP failures occur to generate drop events. The drop_monitor_task receives drop notifications and writes `telemetry.otlp.dropped` events to the fallback FileSink, but the test shows zero events there. This suggests either drop events aren't being sent to the channel, the monitor task isn't writing them, or the file sink itself isn't receiving data. Looking at how the file sink gets passed through the system, the constructor takes an `Option<Arc<Box<dyn Sink>>>` that gets cloned, and the test is wrapping a `Box::new(file_sink)` within an `Arc`.
